2026-05-04.post_text:
Automation just got a whole lot smarter
 
2026 is shaping up to be a critical year for businesses to embrace automation and stay ahead of the curve.
 
• According to a recent survey of 400 IT professionals, automation is shifting from just automating tasks to building secure and trustworthy AI-driven workflow automation.
• In fact, 80% of companies are now focusing on building a resilient and adaptable ecosystem that can evolve as demands change.
• Manufacturers are already seeing the benefits, with connected factory automation increasing production efficiency by up to 30%.
• A recent discussion highlighted the importance of automating emails to free up staff time.
• With the right tools, you can automate up to 90% of your email workload, saving time and resources.

Here are 5 steps to automate your emails:
• Identify repetitive email tasks that can be automated
• Choose an automation tool that integrates with your existing systems
• Set up workflows to trigger automated email responses
• Test and refine your automated email workflows
• Monitor and analyze the results to optimize your automation strategy

By automating your emails, you can free up staff time and focus on higher-value tasks.
 
What's the one email task that's eating up the most time in your business?
 
Learn more about automating your workflow.
 
#AutomationSolutions #AIforBusiness #EmailAutomation

topic.title: Manual Task Automation
topic.why: Asks a question that gets SMB owners talking about AI in their own business

2026-05-04.post_text:
What's eating your time?
 
Manual tasks are a major pain point for many businesses.
They slow you down and take away from more important things.

• Automating just one task can save you hours per week
• Most businesses have at least 5 tasks that can be automated
• The right automation tools can cut your software costs in half

You don't have to do everything yourself, and you shouldn't.
 
Which task is taking up the most time in your operations and how can you streamline it?
 
Let's talk about how to free up more time in your day.
 
#AIforBusiness #AutomationSolutions #SmallBusinessProductivity

topic.title: First AI Automation in 20 Min
topic.angle: 5-step process to automate your most repetitive task — no developer, no code, done before lunch
topic.why: Action-oriented Wednesday content with a clear process SMB owners can start today

2026-05-18.post_text:
What if you could automate a task in 20 minutes?
 
You're probably wasting hours each week on repetitive tasks that eat into your productivity and profits.
 
• Most automations can be set up with no code and no developer.
• You can use pre-built templates to get started quickly.
• The right tools can help you automate tasks in minutes, not hours.
 
The key to successful automation is identifying the right task to start with.
 
Which of these tasks is eating the most time in your business?
 
Try automating one task this week and see the impact for yourself.
What task will you automate first to boost your productivity?
#AIautomation #productivityhacks #automationmadeeasy

topic.title: AI Stack Replaces $3k Software
topic.angle: SMBs replacing $3,000/month in software subscriptions with a $28/month AI stack — here's the breakdown
topic.why: Data-driven Thursday post with specific cost numbers that make owners stop scrolling

2026-05-18.post_text:
A client came to us spending $3,200 a month on software

They replaced it with a $28 AI stack. Here is exactly what that looked like

Manual bookkeeping: 12 hours a week → automated in 40 minutes
Customer support tickets: 3-day response time → instant answers
Email follow-ups: 80% of them now write themselves
CRM data entry: completely eliminated with an AI agent
Reporting dashboards: built once, updates in real time

The $28 stack: ChatGPT Plus ($20/month) + Zapier ($8/month) + custom GPTs

The formula: hours saved per week x your billable rate x 52 weeks = real cost of doing it manually

Most business owners treat software subscriptions like fixed costs

They are not. They are a choice between paying for features you use and paying for outcomes you need

What is the one manual process in your business you have been meaning to automate for months — and what has actually stopped you from doing it

#AI #SmallBusiness
